hypothesis: If a diffusion model trained exclusively on metastable state data is guided during inference using interpolated conditional scores (Score-Based Interpolation) and refined via energy-maximizing force projection along the score-difference reaction coordinate (Score-Aligned Ascent), then it will generate accurate transition state structures without requiring any transition state training data, because the interpolated score functions implicitly encode the reaction pathway geometry and the ascent procedure drives configurations toward the true saddle point region.
